title: Rails + SQLite bidirectional sync via CRDTs (production-oriented blueprint)
goals: Bidirectional, multi-writer sync between two Rails servers using SQLite locally
Sub-second de-sync under normal network conditions
Data consistency with deterministic merges (CRDTs), minimal error risk
Operational reliability with idempotency, backpressure, and observability
principles: Never file-sync SQLite. Replicate logical CRDT operations.
Operation-based CRDTs for efficiency; commutative/idempotent application.
Security by mTLS or HMAC. Exactly-once semantics via idempotency keys.
Continuous streaming transport with retries and watermarking.

architecture.data_modeling_choice: Field-level CRDTs for relational data; document CRDTs for complex nested content

crdts_recommended.scalar_fields: LWW-Register with Lamport timestamp + node-id tiebreaker
crdts_recommended.sets/tags/associations: OR-Set (Observed-Removed Set) with unique add IDs
crdts_recommended.records/deletes: LWW tombstone per record (delete is a CRDT op)
crdts_recommended.text/collaborative_docs: Automerge (document CRDT) via sidecar for rich text or nested JSON

replication.transport: HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2 with keep-alive; optional WebSocket for push
replication.security: mTLS (recommended) or HMAC with per-peer rotating secrets
replication.ordering: Not required; CRDT ops are commutative
replication.watermarking: Per-peer last_sent_op_pk (or lamport) to enable resume

failover.retries: Exponential backoff with jitter; dead-letter after N failures
failover.reconciliation: State converges automatically; ops can be replayed safely

infra_tables.name: crdt_ops
infra_tables.purpose: Durable oplog for all CRDT operations

infra_tables.name: node_clock
infra_tables.seed: INSERT OR IGNORE INTO node_clock (id, lamport) VALUES (1, 0)

lamport_clock.persist: Update node_clock.lamport atomically on every op

lww_register.compare: Higher lamport wins; tie-break by lexicographic node_id
lww_register.apply: If incoming (ts,node) > current, set value and metadata

delete_record.tombstone: LWW-delete: set tombstone=1 with (ts,node) as LWW register

push_endpoint.auth: mTLS (recommended) or HMAC header: X-Signature: hex(HMAC_SHA256(body, key))
notes: Batch up to ~64KB compressed for low latency.
Apply ops transactionally; skip if applied_ops contains op_id.

write_path: - For LWW set: {op_kind: lww_set, payload: {field, value, ts: lamport, node}}
- For OR-Set add: {op_kind: orset_add, payload: {set, element, tag_uid, ts, node}}
- For OR-Set remove: orset_remove referencing tag_uid
Service: persist op to crdt_ops, apply to materialized tables, insert into applied_ops, enqueue PushToPeersJob
apply_logic_pseudocode: - lww_set: compare (lamport,node) vs current_{field}_ts,node; update if newer
- pn_inc: upsert pn_counters with p += delta for node_id
- pn_dec: upsert pn_counters with n += delta for node_id
- delete: set tombstone=1 using LWW compare on tombstone_ts,node

background_jobs.name: PushToPeersJob
background_jobs.trigger: After local commit or periodic batch (e.g., every 100ms)
background_jobs.logic: For each peer, select crdt_ops.rowid > peers.last_sent_op_rowid ORDER BY rowid LIMIT N; POST; on 200, advance watermark

background_jobs.name: RetryDeadLettersJob
background_jobs.logic: Retry failed batches with exponential backoff, max_age configurable

background_jobs.name: CompactionJob
logic: Periodically snapshot materialized state per entity
Prune crdt_ops up to the minimum acknowledged watermark across peers
For OR-Set, hard-delete pairs (add, remove) whose remove is acknowledged by all peers

observability: Metrics: ops_emitted, ops_applied, replication_lag_seconds (now - last_received_at), conflicts_resolved_count
Logs: one line per batch with peer_id, count, latency, success/failure
Health: /healthz includes last_sent_op_rowid per peer and node_clock

recommended_defaults.batching: Up to 200 ops or 64KB compressed, whichever first, every 100–200ms
recommended_defaults.http: HTTP/2 with keep-alive; 2 concurrent in-flight batches per peer
recommended_defaults.security: mTLS preferred; else HMAC with key rotation and short TTL
recommended_defaults.storage_limits: Compaction target keeps < 7 days of ops and last acknowledged watermark

edge_cases_and_guarantees.reordered_delivery: Safe; CRDTs are commutative; LWW uses (lamport,node)
edge_cases_and_guarantees.clock_skew: Harmless; Lamport merges causally on receipt
edge_cases_and_guarantees.network_partitions: Writes proceed independently; convergence on reconnection
edge_cases_and_guarantees.schema_changes: Use expand/contract; unknown fields in payloads are ignored

migration_path: Add CRDT metadata columns (field_ts, field_node, tombstone_ts/node)
Create infra tables (crdt_ops, applied_ops, peers, node_clock)
Implement CRDT apply functions and services
Deploy transport endpoints and background push
Run initial full-state bootstrap (emit ops for current state)
Enable continuous replication and monitor lag
reliability_hardening: Use database transactions to atomically: insert crdt_ops, apply state, insert applied_ops
Circuit breaker on per-peer failures to avoid thundering retries
Backpressure: if peer lag > threshold, reduce batch size and frequency
Dead-letter store with admin UI to inspect and replay
Periodic end-to-end checksum: hash of materialized state per entity to detect drift
when_to_use_document_crdts: If you have nested JSON, lists with positions, or collaborative text
Recommendation: Automerge (Rust/JS) via a small sidecar service; Rails stores snapshots and ops
Keep the same transport, idempotency, and watermarking
non_goals: Strong real-time transactional guarantees across nodes (use a replicated server DB for that)
Row-level referential integrity during out-of-order delivery; handle in apply logic or via post-apply validation
